metrics.narrative: Devesh Chadha framed core success metrics on the discovery call (25 Feb 2026, ~19 weeks ago) as a seamless end-to-end platform serving procurement, legal, and sales, efficient transaction throughput, AI-driven agreement creation, and strict infosec compliance. Contract-volume assumptions underpinning pricing have swung wildly: Jason Lieu flagged the initial 500-contract assumption as understated by orders of magnitude ('I'm hearing, like, hundreds of thousands,' 7 Apr 2026), and a later ~12,500-annual-contract estimate from Melanie Hamm's team was still being reconciled as of 13 Apr 2026 (~12 weeks ago). No quantified ROI case has been confirmed as delivered to the budget-approving Cadence leadership group.

champion.narrative: No individual has emerged as a Zycus advocate. Devesh Chadha sourced the original RFP inclusion (23 Jan 2026) but functions as Decision Maker, not internal champion. Peter Hughes runs point on logistics for both vendors and explicitly told Zycus the parallel Ironclad sandbox 'doesn't have the same constraints' (28 Apr 2026), signaling neutrality rather than advocacy. Legal was reported as early-impressed by Ironclad-style tooling ('iClad,' 23 Jan 2026), and that sentiment has never been countered by later evidence. The recent account-owner transition (~9 Jun 2026) further disrupts any nascent relationship-building.

competition.narrative: Ironclad is the confirmed remaining rival: the 9 Jun 2026 Next Step states Cadence is 'down to 1 of 2 vendors including Ironclad.' The threat traces back to the very first call (23 Jan 2026), where legal's comfort with Ironclad-style tooling was first raised, and it was reinforced on 28 Apr 2026 when Peter Hughes confirmed Cadence was running an equally resourced, more flexible parallel sandbox for that rival. Two smaller intake-tool alternatives ('Tonkin' and 'Yep') were evaluated and explicitly ruled out on cost/capability grounds on that same first call.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly and repeatedly articulated by name: Devesh Chadha described disparate intake via Salesforce and manual email causing inconsistent workflows (25 Feb 2026); Melanie Hamm detailed how Cadence's current coded-template process lives entirely in Salesforce and requires an IT release cycle to update ('it is a pain... to change anything,' 20 Apr 2026); and the buyer flagged a lack of visibility for sales-finance into signed contracts without routing through legal. These are specific, named, buyer-owned pains, not generic complaints.

economic_buyer.narrative: No individual economic buyer has ever appeared on a Zycus call across all 13 deep-read touchpoints spanning 23 Jan to 28 Apr 2026. Budget authority sits with the unnamed Cadence leadership budget owner; the 26 May 2026 Next Step (~6.3 weeks ago) noted this group was meeting 'to listen to final budget requests,' with vendor selection anticipated the first week of June, but no outcome of that meeting or any engagement with that group has been logged since. This is the single largest qualification gap on the deal.

decision_process.narrative: The process has run a structured RFP → demo → workshop → sandbox/POC sequence, with Cadence explicitly running Zycus and Ironclad in parallel through comparable evaluation steps (Peter Hughes, 28 Apr 2026: 'we're running a similar sandbox with the other candidate solution, and we don't have the same constraints'). The process reached a POC (kickoff 12 May 2026 per Next Step History) and a wrap-up meeting (20 May 2026), then a leadership budget review (week of 26 May 2026), but the anticipated 'first week of June' vendor selection did not occur; the 9 Jun 2026 update (~4.3 weeks ago) instead reported the decision pushed to mid-July with the field down to Zycus and Ironclad.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unusually well documented: Melanie Hamm walked through exhaustive coded-template and clause-logic requirements across two dedicated workshops (20 and 21 Apr 2026, ~11 weeks ago), Cadence IT ran a full architecture/integration review covering SAP ECC6, Salesforce, iManage, SSO, and multi-tenancy (6 Apr 2026, ~13.5 weeks ago), and Devesh Chadha separately required validated Peppol invoice processing for Belgium, France, and Italy (28 Apr 2026, ~10.4 weeks ago). These sessions give Zycus a detailed, buyer-validated map of what a winning solution must do.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cadence named Zycus one of a final two (with Ironclad) on 9 Jun 2026, but the internal budget/vendor-selection decision that was due early June has slipped to mid-July, the Zycus account changed owners (Kevin Cipollaro to Karson Keogh, ~9 Jun 2026) with no confirmed live buyer session since, and the close date of 10 Jul 2026 (2 days from today) has no supporting contracting activity behind it.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (or push Close Date to align with the buyer's stated mid-July vendor-selection decision plus a realistic 3-6 week contracting window)

flags: Ironclad remains a live, credible alternative with an unresolved early legal preference (Devesh Chadha, 23 Jan); pair Melanie Hamm with a Zycus legal-ops reference customer before the mid-July vote to neutralize it.

No economic buyer has ever taken a Zycus call; use the newly-opened Palo Alto executive-event invite as the vehicle to finally reach Cadence's budget-approving leadership before the vendor choice is finalized.
No champion with EB access or advocacy. Peter Hughes (Evaluator) is a coordinator, not an advocate. Devesh Chadha (Decision Maker) newly engaged, passive, no evidence of push for Zycus.

No champion. Peter Hughes (procurement project lead, Evaluator contact role) is closest candidate but has not demonstrated advocacy. His last call was 21 Apr; since then referenced only in third person in Next_Step. No one actively selling Zycus internally or countering legal's Ironclad preference.

Close date 10 Jul is 2 days out; decision pushed to mid-July (9 Jun Next_Step). No contracting activity since MSA submission 21 Apr (no redlines, no SOW, no PO). Minimum 1-week post-decision slip to signature, realistic 3-4 weeks for full legal review. Close will slip to late Jul or early Aug.
Best Case forecast with no EB engagement, no champion, live Ironclad competition, and decision not yet made is indefensible. Recommended forecast: Pipeline.

The early-June rep handoff risks losing the momentum built across six months of workshops; the new deal owner should personally reconnect with Peter Hughes and Devesh Chadha now rather than rely on the Palo Alto event invite alone.

The account changed owners mid-cycle (Kevin Cipollaro to Karson Keogh, ~9 Jun 2026) with no confirmed live session since; rebuild continuity with Peter Hughes now, using the Palo Alto event as the reconnect vehicle, before the final-two decision is made without a de facto Zycus voice in the room.
best_practice_check.summary: The highest-leverage remaining plays are closing the executive-access gap and building a genuine champion before the mid-July decision, given Ironclad's unrebutted early advantage with legal.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer has voiced a preference for Zycus over Ironclad in any read call; Peter Hughes describes Cadence's evaluation as parallel and equally weighted for both vendors, 28 Apr 2026.

competitive_position.summary: Ironclad is the confirmed, live remaining rival in a final two-vendor decision, with an unrebutted early legal preference and a more flexible parallel evaluation track. Coupa is the incumbent CLM being displaced, not a forward threat. Two smaller intake-tool alternatives were ruled out early on cost/capability grounds.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This deal shows genuine evaluation depth (deep technical workshops, a five-day sandbox, an architecture deep-dive) but is structurally exposed: no economic buyer has ever engaged, no internal champion has emerged, a real competitor remains in the final two with an unrebutted early advantage, the decision date has slipped three times, and a fresh account-owner transition has yet to produce a live buyer conversation. The close date on record is not credible against this evidence.

deal_risk.text: Close date is 10 Jul 2026 (tomorrow), but the buyer's own 9 Jun 2026 update pushed the decision to mid-July with no vendor chosen and no contracting motion (SOW, redlines, PO) visible since the MSA was sent 21 Apr 2026.
The true budget approver has never been reached by Zycus at any point across the deal's ~5.5-month history.

win_position.text: The buyer completed a full multi-week validation motion (technical workshops 20-21 Apr 2026, a 5-day extended sandbox, an integration deep-dive 6 Apr 2026), showing real, Zycus-specific evaluation effort.
No economic buyer has ever appeared on a Zycus call; budget approval sits with the unnamed Cadence leadership budget owner, whose 26 May 2026 meeting outcome was never confirmed back to Zycus.

Cadence remains actively split between Zycus and Ironclad as of the 9 Jun 2026 Next Step ('down to 1 of 2 vendors including Ironclad'), and legal's early comfort with Ironclad-style tooling (23 Jan 2026) has never been countered.

No internal champion has emerged after more than five months; Peter Hughes told Zycus directly (28 Apr 2026) that Cadence is running a parallel, less-constrained sandbox for 'the other candidate solution.'

deal_momentum.text: The vendor decision has slipped from 'end of April' (1 Apr 2026) to 'early June' (26 May 2026) to 'mid-July' (9 Jun 2026), with no confirmed outcome as of today.

The account owner changed around 9 Jun 2026 per a transition note from the deal owner's manager to Peter Hughes, and the new owner has not yet logged a live call with the buyer as of the most recent update, 29 Jun 2026.
A genuine two-way buyer email reply was logged 19 Jun 2026, and Devesh Chadha's team was invited to and is still weighing attendance at a Zycus Palo Alto event per the 16 Jun and 29 Jun 2026 notes.

customer_commitment.text: Cadence invested real time in a five-day sandbox and multiple deep workshops in April 2026.
Peter Hughes confirmed on 28 Apr 2026 that Cadence is running an equally resourced, less-constrained parallel evaluation of Ironclad at the same time.

hard.ais_why:
• The 95-minute 4/3/2026 workshop generated 22 action items. Devika Sanil's commitment to send Jason Lieu and the team the list of external data sources behind the AI risk model is a buyer-pulled ask, and Kevin Cipollaro's cost breakdown is split into CLM, Intake, and iRisk line items, confirming Merlin Intake and AI-driven iRisk are inside the priced scope.
• Other buyer-pulled commitments include the SAP adapter intro to Devesh Chadha, sub-tier mapping plus FedRAMP authorization status, the product roadmap going to Jason and Devesh, and a standard-versus-premium support table. Cadence is driving the agenda. The earlier 1/25/2026 rep note that Cadence is unimpressed with Coupa's Agentic AI is the competitive opening Zycus is now stepping into.
• POC kickoff is scheduled for 5/12/2026, the MSA went in on 4/21, two references provided, and the project lead has told the team Zycus is rated higher than the other vendor. Transcript-level keyword review confirmed AI and Merlin signal present; champion, POC scope including Intake and iRisk, and budget alignment justify High confidence.
